key_insight: NEAT automates neoantigen triage for personalized cancer vaccine design, using machine learning to prioritize peptide candidates and reduce manual review bottlenecks.
clinical_relevance: This could speed and scale personalized cancer vaccine workflows by helping teams move faster from patient sequencing data to vaccine manufacturing.
method: Trained a machine learning model on prior vaccine-design outcomes from 33 patients and 1,943 peptides across 3 clinical trials
Used features including tumor variant allele frequency, RNA expression, driver gene status, binding/presentation scores, and transcript support level
Classified candidates as accept, reject, or needs human review and integrated the model into pVACtools v7.0.0
evidence_signal: The model was evaluated on existing vaccine design results and reported strong performance with 0.847 sensitivity, 0.924 specificity, and an AUC of 0.955.
watch_item: Performance comes from retrospective trial data, so real-world generalizability and the need for expert oversight in borderline cases still matter.
paper_title: Automating neoantigen selection for personalized cancer vaccine design
